meta.source: CMS GDPR Enforcement Tracker (enforcementtracker.com), EDPB, national DPA publications
meta.note: Contains publicly documented GDPR fines only. Not exhaustive — fines not made public are excluded.

Facebook.com.summary: Unlawful transfer of EU user data to the United States without adequate safeguards.
Data scraping breach notification failures. Over 533 million users' data exposed.
Multiple data breaches between June 2018 and December 2019. Insufficient technical measures.

Instagram.com.summary: Processing of children's personal data, including public display of phone numbers and email addresses of minors.
Unlawful use of 'legitimate interests' as legal basis for behavioural advertising.

TikTok.com.summary: Unlawful transfer of European user data to China. Failure to meet transparency requirements.
Failure to protect children's data. Default public settings for minors, insufficient age verification.

LinkedIn.com.summary: Unlawful processing of personal data for behavioural advertising. Failure to provide transparent information about legal bases.

Google.com.summary: Making it harder for users to refuse cookies than to accept them. Dark patterns in consent interface.
YouTube cookie consent failures — refusing cookies made more difficult than accepting.
Lack of transparent information and valid consent for personalised advertising on Android devices.

YouTube.com.summary: Cookie consent failures — refusing cookies made more difficult than accepting.

WhatsApp.com.summary: Lack of transparency about data processing, including data sharing with Facebook. Insufficient privacy notice.

Amazon (EU domains).summary: Largest GDPR fine at the time. Unlawful processing for advertising purposes. Consent mechanisms deemed insufficient.

Twitter/X.com.summary: Failure to notify the DPA of a personal data breach within 72 hours.

Uber.com.summary: Unlawful transfer of European drivers' personal data to the United States without adequate safeguards.
Failure to report a personal data breach within 72 hours. 57 million records exposed.

Spotify.com.summary: Failure to provide users with clear and comprehensible information when exercising right of access.

Airbnb.com.summary: Insufficient transparency about data processing for advertising purposes and third-party sharing.

Booking.com.summary: Failure to report a personal data breach (access by fraudsters to hotel partners) within 72 hours.

Deliveroo.co.uk.summary: Insufficient information provided to delivery workers about processing of their personal data.

Easyjet.com.summary: ICO investigation into 2020 data breach affecting 9 million customers. No formal fine issued — regulatory action taken.

Snapchat.com.summary: Failure to adequately assess privacy risks of its 'My AI' feature to children.

Discord.com.summary: Retention of personal data beyond necessary periods. Inadequate information about data retention. Security failures.

Bumble.com.summary: Excessive data collection and insufficient data minimisation in profile creation. Privacy by design failures.

Badoo.com.summary: Excessive data collection and insufficient data minimisation — same enforcement action as Bumble (same parent company).
